user_query:
I'm organizing a WWII-themed book club quiz for my classmates, and I want to make sure the questions are accurate and clearly worded. I put my draft and basic rules in the workspace:
- input/draft_quiz.md (10 questions with IDs, tags, and text)
- input/rules.yaml (allowed source domains, word limits, and output paths)

What I need you to do:
1) Fact-check only the questions tagged FACT_CHECK using live Internet search:
- Use a search engine to find evidence; do not navigate directly without a query. Record the exact query text used for each source.
- For each fact-checked question, confirm it as "plausible" or mark "needs_revision" based on at least two independent sources whose domains are listed under source_domains_allowed in input/rules.yaml.
- For each source, save: domain (just the domain, no full URL), page_title, and the exact query_used that found it. Do not include full URLs in outputs.
- Output a single JSON array to out/validation/quiz_fact_check.json with objects like: {"id","original_text","decision":"plausible|needs_revision","reason","sources":[{"domain","page_title","query_used"}, ...]}. The number of records must equal the number of FACT_CHECK items in input/draft_quiz.md.
- Also write out/validation/queries.txt containing the unique set of all query_used strings from the JSON, one per line sorted alphabetically. The set must match exactly what appears in the JSON.

2) Rewrite all 10 questions for clarity and brevity for a 9th-grade audience:
- Follow the limits in input/rules.yaml (max_question_words and grade_level_hint). Keep each rewritten question within the word limit.
- Preserve each question's ID and start each line with "ID: " followed by the rewritten question.
- For any question tagged SPOILER_SENSITIVE, avoid revealing plot specifics; phrase it as general knowledge or mark it optional.
- Save the rewritten set to out/quiz_rewritten.md and also to out/quiz_rewritten.csv with columns: id, rewritten_question, word_count.

3) Draft a short email to my history teacher requesting to use the fact-checked quiz in our book club session:
- Address the recipient from input/rules.yaml (email_recipient) and include a subject line.
- Keep it under the email_word_limit in the rules and use a friendly, respectful tone.
- Briefly summarize how many items were fact-checked and how many need revision (tally from your JSON), mention the attached files by their exact paths, and ask for permission/feedback.
- Save to out/email/email_to_teacher.txt.

Notes and constraints:
- Only use domains listed in input/rules.yaml when citing sources. Each FACT_CHECK item must have at least two sources from the allowed list, with recorded queries.
- Do not include any URLs in your outputs—just domain names and page titles.
- All paths above are required. Ensure file counts, IDs, and tags align between inputs and outputs.

Please proceed and produce the three deliverables at the specified paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
